name: Aman Mishra
title: AI Systems Engineer
mission: Engineering Autonomous Intelligence for the Real World.
philosophy: I don't build AI demos. I build systems that operate under real constraints — latency budgets, failure modes, state management, and observability requirements.
Every architecture decision I make is grounded in production reality: what happens when the LLM times out, when the vector search returns empty, when state gets corrupted mid-conversation.
I think in systems. Not in models.

approach.label: How I think
approach.text: From constraints backward to architecture. Never from technology forward to use case.

approach.label: How I build
approach.text: Instrument first. Optimize after. Reliability is a feature, not an afterthought.

approach.label: Why production quality matters
approach.text: A demo that works once is interesting. A system that works ten thousand times is engineering.
